content:
# elizaos-plugins/plugin-solana Daily Update (Apr 1, 2026)
## OVERVIEW 
Today's activity focused on discussions around integrating external AI marketplace and trust layer solutions, with significant updates on MoltBridge and the proposal of a new MAXIA plugin.

## KEY TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENTS

No new technical developments were completed today.

## NEWLY OPENED PULL REQUESTS
No new pull requests were opened today.

## CLOSED ISSUES
No issues were closed today.

## NEW ISSUES
No new issues were opened today.

## ACTIVE ISSUES

### Exploring AI Marketplace and Trust Layer Integrations
Discussions continued on several proposals for integrating external services to enhance ElizaOS agents.

*   **AgentID for Cryptographic Identity & Trust Layer** ([#6688](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-solana/issues/6688)): A new comment from majorelalexis-stack highlighted that MAXIA implements W3C DIDs with AIP v0.3.0 (ed25519) and enforces trust levels L0-L4 with spend caps on Solana/Base, suggesting it natively solves the ElizaOS trust gap. This follows an earlier comment from haroldmalikfrimpong-ops emphasizing AgentID's role in providing cryptographic identity, trust levels, and blockchain receipts for agent marketplaces.
*   **MoltBridge Trust & Discovery Layer** ([#6501](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-solana/issues/6501)): JKHeadley provided an update, noting MoltBridge's inclusion in the official A2A registry and the stability of its API. The comment also mentioned that CrewAI has adopted similar trust framing with `trust_score` and `is_verified` on their agent model, validating the need for such a layer. The agent card is live for interop testing, and the team is ready to assist with an ElizaOS plugin.
*   **MAXIA AI Marketplace Plugin Proposal** ([#6700](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-solana/issues/6700)): majorelalexis-stack confirmed that a plugin implementation for MAXIA is ready, including 5 actions (MAXIA_DISCOVER, MAXIA_GET_PRICE, MAXIA_SWAP, MAXIA_RENT_GPU, MAXIA_BUY_SERVICE) and a MAXIA_MARKET_CONTEXT provider. The implementation is TypeScript strict, has zero build errors, and includes a sandbox mode for testing, with an offer to submit a PR to the plugin registry.
